tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

The new Skills APIs are not mentioned at all. - **README has no Skills section**: The README Table of Contents and Examples section cover many tools (Web Search, Bing, MCP, OpenAPI, etc.) but has no entry for Skills. - **No sample file**: There is no `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/samples/` file covering Skills. - **No test-backed snippet**: There is no `tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs` (or equivalent) with `#region Snippet:` tags wired to a README placeholder.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — replace the sparse 2.1.0-beta.1 entry with a richer one: ```markdown ## 2.1.0-beta.1 (2026-04-21) ### Features Added - Added Skills support for the Responses API. Use `ResponsesInlineSkillParam` to embed a skill inline (as a base64-encoded zip bundle) or `ResponsesSkillReferenceParam` to reference a pre-published skill by ID. Local skill directories can be described with `LocalSkillParam`. - The sample for Hosted agent was updated. ``` #### 1b. Add Skills section to README Table of Contents **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md` **Action:** Edit — in the Table of Contents, add an entry after `Memory search tool` and before `Azure Function tool`: ```markdown - [Skills](#skills) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### 2a. Write the sample test file **Create:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Skills.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.IO; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using NUnit.Framework; using OpenAI; namespace Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI.Tests.Samples { public partial class ExtensionsOpenAISamples : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] public void SkillReference() { #region Snippet:SkillReference ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new( endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint), tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential()); var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient(); // Reference a pre-published skill by its ID (and optionally a version). var skillRef = new ResponsesSkillReferenceParam(skillId: "") { Version = "latest" }; var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions(); options.Tools.Add(skillRef); var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse( model: "gpt-4o", input: "Use the skill to answer: what is the capital of France?", options: options); Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] public void InlineSkill() { #region Snippet:InlineSkill ProjectOpenAIClient projectClient = new( endpoint: new Uri(TestEnvironment.ProjectEndpoint), tokenProvider: new Azure.Identity.DefaultAzureCredential()); var responsesClient = projectClient.GetProjectResponsesClient(); // Read a zip bundle that contains the skill implementation. byte[] skillZipBytes = File.ReadAllBytes("path/to/skill.zip"); string base64Zip = Convert.ToBase64String(skillZipBytes); var inlineSkill = new ResponsesInlineSkillParam( name: "my-inline-skill", description: "A custom skill embedded inline", source: new ResponsesInlineSkillSourceParam(data: base64Zip)); var options = new OpenAI.Responses.ResponseCreationOptions(); options.Tools.Add(inlineSkill); var response = responsesClient.CreateResponse( model: "gpt-4o", input: "Use the skill to process my request.", options: options); Console.WriteLine(response.Value.OutputText); #endregion } } } ``` #### 2b. Add README snippet placeholders **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add the following **Skills** section after the `Memory search tool` section and before the `Azure Function tool` section: ````markdown ### Skills Skills are reusable capabilities that can be attached to a Responses API request either by **referencing** a pre-published skill or by providing the skill **inline** as a zip bundle. #### Reference a published skill ```C# Snippet:SkillReference ``` #### Embed a skill inline ```C# Snippet:InlineSkill ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Skills** entry is missing from the Examples subsection and needs to be added as described above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — injects snippet code from the new test file into README placeholders. Verify the README diff shows the Skills code blocks populated. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — regenerates public API listing files (not needed here since no new public API was added, but run to confirm). 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`

AI Extensions OpenAI] Docs: Document expanded tool call tracing support in OpenTelemetry section","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/` **Triggered by:** PR #57821 (commit 6990ac18d) by `@M-Hietala` ## What Changed PR #57821 expanded `OpenTelemetryResponseScope.cs` to emit trace spans and attributes for additional tool call types used in the OpenAI Responses API: - `code_interpreter_call` — captures `code`, `outputs`, `status` - `file_search_call` — captures `queries`, `results`, `status` - `web_search_call` — captures `action`, `status` - `mcp_call` / `mcp_*` variants — captures `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, `status` New telemetry test files were also added: `ResponsesTelemetryTests.CodeInterpreter.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.FileSearch.cs`, `ResponsesTelemetryTests.Mcp.cs`, and `ResponsesTelemetryTests.WebSearch.cs`. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG** (`CHANGELOG.md`): The `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` "Features Added" section is empty. The expanded tool call tracing should be listed here. - **README tracing section** (`README.md`): The existing "Tracing" section covers setup (enabling GenAI tracing, content recording, Azure Monitor / console exporters) but does not document *which tool types* are instrumented or *what span attributes* are captured per type. Users have no guidance on what trace data they can expect when using Code Interpreter, File Search, Web Search, or MCP tools. - **No sample demonstrating tool tracing**: `tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` only shows a simple text-prompt trace. There is no snippet showing how tool-call spans appear in traces, which the README could link to.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section: ```markdown ## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Expanded OpenTelemetry tracing support to additional tool call types in the Responses API: - `code_interpreter_call` — traces code execution with `code`, `outputs`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled) - `file_search_call` — traces file search operations with `queries`, `results`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled) - `web_search_call` — traces web search calls with `action` and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled) - `mcp_call` and `mcp_*` variants — traces MCP tool calls with `name`, `arguments`, `server_label`, and `status` fields (when content recording is enabled) - Generic fallback tracing for any unrecognized `*_call` output item types ``` #### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/README.md` Add a new subsection **"Traced tool types"** inside the existing `### Tracing` section, after the "Enabling content recording" subsection and before `## Troubleshooting`. Insert: ```markdown ### Traced tool types When GenAI tracing is enabled, the SDK emits a `gen_ai.tool.message` event on the response span for each tool call present in the response. The following tool types are supported: | Tool type | Always captured | Captured when content recording is enabled | |---|---|---| | `function_call` | call ID | function name, arguments | | `code_interpreter_call` | call ID, status | code, outputs | | `file_search_call` | call ID, status | queries, results | | `web_search_call` | call ID, status | action | | `mcp_call` | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments | | `mcp_*` (other MCP variants) | call ID, server label, status | name, arguments, input, output | > **Note:** Binary data such as images or generated files is never captured in traces regardless of content recording settings. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new README snippet is strictly required for the CHANGELOG fix. However, optionally add a snippet demonstrating a response that triggers tool calls, to show what appears in traces. If added: 1. Add a test method in `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/tests/Samples/Sample_Responses_Tracing.cs` wrapping tool-call tracing setup with `#region Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` / `#endregion`. 2. Insert a ```` ```C# Snippet:Sample_ResponsesToolCallTracing` ```` placeholder in the README after the new "Traced tool types" table. This is optional — the table in the README already describes what is traced without requiring a live code sample. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing Only the content within the "Tracing" subsection and the CHANGELOG need updating. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — only needed if a new snippet was added in Step 2 4. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/`

foundry *_call/*_call_output items can't be sent as Responses input; Conversations API returns undocumented remote_function_call instead","body":"# [BUG] Foundry agent tool call types have dual discriminator and block stateless multi-turn replay ## Library name and version Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2 (transitively pulls Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1, OpenAI 2.9.1) ## Describe the bug Two related issues with how Azure-specific agent tool call types (e.g. `openapi_call`) surface through the Responses and Conversations APIs: ### 1. Stateless multi-turn replay fails for Azure tool types When using `ProjectResponsesClient` with an agent that has Azure-specific tools (OpenAPI, AI Search, Bing Grounding), `ResponseResult.OutputItems` contains items with `"type": "openapi_call"`. Attempting to replay these as `InputItems` in a follow-up request (stateless multi-turn) results in HTTP 400: ``` HTTP 400 (invalid_request_error: invalid_value) Parameter: input[1] Invalid value: 'openapi_call'. Supported values are: 'apply_patch_call', 'apply_patch_call_output', 'code_interpreter_call', 'compaction', 'computer_call', 'computer_call_output', 'custom_tool_call', 'custom_tool_call_output', 'file_search_call', 'function_call', 'function_call_output', 'image_generation_call', 'item_reference', 'local_shell_call', 'local_shell_call_output', 'mcp_approval_request', 'mcp_approval_response', 'mcp_call', 'mcp_list_tools', 'message', 'reasoning', 'shell_call', 'shell_call_output', 'tool_search_call', 'tool_search_output', and 'web_search_call'. ``` This forces users into server-managed state (`PreviousResponseId` or Conversations API) — there's no way to do stateless multi-turn with Azure-specific tools. ### 2. Dual discriminator: Conversations API returns undocumented type The same logical operation (an OpenAPI tool call) surfaces with different `type` values depending on the API: | API | JSON `type` value | SDK deserialization result | |---|---|---| | Responses API `output[]` | `openapi_call` | [`InternalUnknownItemResource`](https://github.com/openai/openai-dotnet/blob/[REDACTED]/src/Generated/Models/Responses/ResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L158) (OpenAI SDK doesn't know it) | | Conversations API items | `remote_function_call` (with `"label": "remote_openapi"`) | [`UnknownAgentResponseItem`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) (Azure SDK doesn't know it either) | `remote_function_call` is **not in the published [Foundry OpenAPI spec](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/[REDACTED]/specification/ai-foundry/data-plane/Foundry/openapi3)** (`microsoft-foundry-openapi3.json` has `openapi_call` but no `remote_function_call`), yet the Conversations endpoint returns it. The [`AgentResponseItem` deserializer](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/src/Generated/Models/AgentResponseItem.Serialization.cs#L226) has a case for `openapi_call` but not `remote_function_call`, so Conversations items with tool calls fall through to `UnknownAgentResponseItem`. ## Expected behavior 1. **Stateless replay should work**: `OutputItems` from a Responses API call should be directly replayable as `InputItems` in a subsequent request without requiring `PreviousResponseId`. The server should accept `openapi_call` as input. 2. **Consistent discriminator**: The same tool call should use the same `type` discriminator across both Responses and Conversations APIs, matching the published OpenAPI spec. `GetProjectConversationItemsAsync` should return typed `AgentResponseItem` subtypes (e.g. `OpenAPIToolCall`), not `UnknownAgentResponseItem`. ## Actual behavior 1. Replaying `OutputItems` as `InputItems` → HTTP 400 rejecting `openapi_call` as input type. 2. Conversations API returns `remote_function_call` (undocumented) instead of `openapi_call`, which deserializes as `UnknownAgentResponseItem`. ## Reproduction Steps Minimal repro for issue 1 (stateless replay failure): ```csharp using Azure.Identity; using Azure.AI.Projects; using Azure.AI.Projects.Agents; using Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI; using OpenAI.Responses; var projectEndpoint = ""; var modelDeploymentName = "gpt-4o-mini"; AIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential()); // Create agent with OpenAPI tool string specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "weather_openapi.json"); OpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new( name: "get_weather", specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)), authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails()); OpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef); PromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName) { Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant.", Tools = { openapiTool } }; AgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion("myAgent", new(agentDef)); ProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent( new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version)); // First request works ResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse("What is the weather in Seattle?"); Console.WriteLine(response.GetOutputText()); // Stateless follow-up: replay OutputItems as InputItems → HTTP 400 var opts = new CreateResponseOptions(); opts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem("What is the weather in Seattle?")); foreach (var item in response.OutputItems) opts.InputItems.Add(item); // Contains openapi_call items opts.InputItems.Add(ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem("What about Portland?")); ResponseResult followUp = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts); // THROWS: HTTP 400 // Cleanup projectClient.Agents.DeleteAgentVersion(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version); ``` Minimal repro for issue 2 (dual discriminator — `remote_function_call` instead of `openapi_call`): ```csharp using Azure.Identity; using Azure.AI.Projects; using Azure.AI.Projects.Agents; using Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI; using OpenAI.Responses; var projectEndpoint = ""; var modelDeploymentName = "gpt-4o-mini"; AIProjectClient projectClient = new(new Uri(projectEndpoint), new DefaultAzureCredential()); // Create agent with OpenAPI tool (same setup as issue 1) string specPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "weather_openapi.json"); OpenApiFunctionDefinition toolDef = new( name: "get_weather", specificationBytes: BinaryData.FromBytes(File.ReadAllBytes(specPath)), authentication: new OpenAPIAnonymousAuthenticationDetails()); OpenAPITool openapiTool = new(toolDef); PromptAgentDefinition agentDef = new(model: modelDeploymentName) { Instructions = "You are a helpful assistant.", Tools = { openapiTool } }; AgentVersion agentVersion = projectClient.Agents.CreateAgentVersion( agentName: "myAgent", options: new AgentVersionCreationOptions(agentDef)); // Create a conversation and send a query via Responses API var conversationsClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectConversationsClient(); ProjectConversation conversation = await conversationsClient.CreateProjectConversationAsync(); ProjectResponsesClient responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent( new AgentReference(agentVersion.Name, agentVersion.Version)); CreateResponseOptions opts = new() { InputItems = { ResponseItem.CreateUserMessageItem("What is the weather in Seattle?") }, }; opts.AgentConversationId = conversation.Id; ResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(opts); // Retrieve conversation items — tool calls come back as "remote_function_call" await foreach (AgentResponseItem item in conversationsClient.GetProjectConversationItemsAsync(conversation.Id)) { // All items are UnknownAgentResponseItem — none resolve to OpenApiToolCall // because the server returns "type": "remote_function_call" (not "openapi_call") Console.WriteLine($"{item.GetType().Name}"); // Prints: UnknownAgentResponseItem (×4) } // Cleanup await {"total_count":81,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute ### Actual behavior

Any use of VisualStudioCredentials with the latest Azure.AI.Projects or Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent fails which makes the use of DefaultAzureCredentials difficult in test and development.

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

Sometimes (not always) when I iterate over `AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate>` I get an error stating:

```
The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true. LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
```

* I have the same problem with both `gpt-4.1` and `gpt-5`
* **Worth pointing out that it works if I use the same agent and prompt from the AI Foundry Agent  playground**

The error comes after `UpdateKind` = RunInProgress

```csharp
PersistentThreadMessage messageResponse = _client.Messages.CreateMessage(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    role: MessageRole.User,
    content: request.Prompt);

AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> stream = _client.Runs.CreateRunStreamingAsync(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    agentId: _agent.Id
);

await foreach (StreamingUpdate streamingUpdate in stream) // This is where the error occurs after RunInProgress
{
      _logger.LogInformation(streamingUpdate.UpdateKind.ToString());
}
```

### Expected behavior

If this is due to a problem with the payload, such as an empty response from the LLM or a throttling error, I would expect to see this error

### Actual behavior

I assume the response from the LLM is not in an expected format and that this is an internal bug.
